name: Write a LinkedIn Post
goal: Create a professional LinkedIn post for executives and thought leaders. Open with a compelling hook or insight. Structure: 3-5 short paragraphs (not bullets), include personal story or data point, key takeaway, thought-provoking question to drive engagement. Constraints: NO emojis, NO hashtags in body (max 3 at end), avoid clickbait, 150-300 words. End with clear call-to-action or discussion prompt. Avoid: sales language, buzzwords like 'game-changer' or 'disruptive', em dashes.
tone: Authoritative and professional, first-person perspective

name: Respond to an Email
goal: Draft a polite and effective email response for business communication. Structure: professional greeting, acknowledge sender's message/question, provide clear response with specific details, next steps or action items, professional closing. Constraints: NO emojis, 150-300 words, avoid contractions in formal contexts. Include: specific answers to questions asked, clear timeline for follow-ups if applicable. Avoid: overly casual language, em dashes, multiple exclamation marks.
tone: Formal and structured, match sender's formality level

name: Summarize an Article
goal: Provide a concise executive summary of the article for busy professionals. Structure: opening sentence stating main thesis, 3-4 key points with supporting evidence, conclusion with implications or takeaways. Constraints: 200-300 words, maintain factual accuracy, NO opinions unless clearly labeled, NO emojis. Include: specific data/statistics from article, author's main arguments. Avoid: direct quotes (paraphrase instead), personal interpretation, em dashes.
tone: Neutral and objective, informative and concise

name: Generate a Blog Post
goal: Write an engaging, SEO-optimized blog post for general audience. Structure: compelling hook in first paragraph, clear H2/H3 subheadings every 200-300 words, 3-5 main sections, actionable conclusion with call-to-action. Constraints: 800-1200 words, 8th grade readability, NO emojis. Include: 2-3 concrete examples, practical takeaways, transition sentences between sections. Use: active voice, second person ('you'), short paragraphs (3-4 sentences max). Avoid: walls of text, em dashes, jargon without explanation.
tone: Friendly and conversational, approachable yet informative

name: Create a Product Description
goal: Craft a compelling product description that drives conversions. Structure: attention-grabbing opening line highlighting main benefit, 2-3 paragraphs covering features-as-benefits, specifications in scannable format, strong closing with urgency or social proof. Constraints: 150-250 words, focus on customer benefits not just features, NO emojis. Include: specific use cases, who this product is for, what problem it solves. Avoid: generic descriptions, em dashes, unsubstantiated claims.
tone: Persuasive and confident, benefit-driven and second-person

name: Draft a Cover Letter
goal: Write a tailored cover letter that demonstrates fit for the specific role. Structure: opening paragraph stating position and hook explaining interest, 2-3 paragraphs matching experience to job requirements with specific examples and quantifiable achievements, closing with enthusiasm and clear call-to-action. Constraints: 300-400 words, NO emojis, NO generic templates. Include: specific company research, concrete examples with metrics, keywords from job description. Avoid: repeating resume verbatim, em dashes, clichés like 'team player' without evidence.
tone: Confident and professional, enthusiastic but not desperate

name: Summarize Meeting Notes
goal: Summarize meeting notes into actionable format for team distribution. Structure: brief context (meeting purpose, attendees, date), key discussion points organized by topic, decisions made, action items with owners and deadlines, next steps. Constraints: bullet points format, 200-400 words, NO emojis, objective tone. Include: specific names for action item owners, concrete deadlines. Use: present tense for decisions, future tense for action items. Avoid: verbatim transcription, personal opinions, ambiguous assignments.
tone: Concise and direct, objective and action-oriented

name: Translate Text
goal: Translate text accurately while preserving meaning, tone, and cultural context. Constraints: maintain original formatting, preserve proper nouns and technical terms unless target language has standard equivalent, match formality level of source text. Include: cultural adaptations where direct translation would be confusing. Preserve: numbers, dates, brand names. Avoid: word-for-word translation that loses meaning, adding interpretation, changing sentence structure unnecessarily.
tone: Neutral and faithful to source material, match original formality

name: Create a Social Media Caption
goal: Write an engaging social media caption optimized for Instagram/Facebook. Structure: hook in first sentence (before 'see more' cutoff), main message in 2-3 sentences, call-to-action or question to drive engagement. Constraints: 125-150 characters for hook, total 150-300 words, emojis ENCOURAGED (2-4 relevant ones). Include: relevant hashtags (5-10 for Instagram), question or CTA at end. Use: line breaks for readability, relatable language. Avoid: hashtag stuffing, multiple exclamation marks in a row, em dashes.
tone: Casual and witty, engaging and second-person

name: Generate Interview Questions
goal: Create a comprehensive set of behavioral and technical interview questions. Structure: 3-4 screening questions, 5-7 behavioral questions using STAR method format, 5-7 technical/role-specific questions, 2-3 scenario-based questions. Constraints: 15-20 total questions, NO illegal questions, bias-free language. Include: mix of experience levels, competency-based questions. Format: question followed by [evaluation note] indicating what to listen for. Avoid: yes/no questions, leading questions, questions that favor certain demographics.
tone: Authoritative and professional, clear and structured

name: Write a Press Release
goal: Draft a media-ready press release following AP style guidelines. Structure: compelling headline (10-12 words), dateline, strong lead paragraph with who/what/when/where/why, 2-3 body paragraphs with supporting details and quotes, boilerplate company description, media contact information. Constraints: 400-600 words, inverted pyramid structure, NO emojis, NO marketing hype, third-person only. Include: at least one executive quote, specific data/statistics. Avoid: superlatives without evidence, em dashes, burying the lead.
tone: Formal and factual, newsworthy and third-person

name: Summarize a Research Paper
goal: Provide a rigorous summary of research paper for academic and technical audiences. Structure: research question/hypothesis, methodology overview, primary findings with quantitative results, statistical significance, limitations, practical implications. Constraints: 400-600 words, maintain technical accuracy, cite specific figures, NO emojis. Include: key metrics (p-values, confidence intervals), technical terminology, comparison to prior research. Avoid: speculation beyond paper scope, casual tone, editorializing on findings.
tone: Precise and technical, objective and scholarly

name: Create a Lesson Plan
goal: Develop a comprehensive lesson plan following educational best practices. Structure: learning objectives using Bloom's taxonomy verbs, materials needed, warm-up activity (5-10 min), main instruction with guided practice (20-30 min), independent practice (15-20 min), assessment method, closure/reflection (5 min). Constraints: 45-60 minute class period, differentiation strategies, NO emojis. Include: specific timing, formative assessment checkpoints, accommodation suggestions. Avoid: vague objectives like 'understand', activities without clear learning purpose.
tone: Instructive and supportive, clear and structured

name: Write a Tweet Thread
goal: Compose an engaging Twitter thread (6-10 tweets). Structure: Tweet 1 hooks with bold statement or question, Tweets 2-8 develop one clear point each with examples or data, final tweet summarizes with CTA. Constraints: each tweet under 280 characters, emojis ENCOURAGED (1-2 per tweet), use line breaks within tweets. Include: relevant hashtags (2-3 total across thread), numbers or statistics. Format: number tweets (1/10, 2/10, etc.). Avoid: walls of text, threads longer than 12 tweets, em dashes.
tone: Casual and punchy, direct and second-person

name: Generate FAQs
goal: Create a comprehensive FAQ list that preemptively addresses customer questions. Structure: 8-12 question-answer pairs organized by category, start with most common questions. Constraints: questions phrased as customers would ask them, answers 2-4 sentences each, NO emojis. Include: specific details (pricing, timelines, specs), troubleshooting steps for common issues. Format: Q: clear question, A: complete answer. Avoid: vague answers like 'it depends', technical jargon without explanation, missing critical steps.
tone: Clear and helpful, empathetic and jargon-free

name: Write a Video Script
goal: Draft a compelling video script for promotional content. Structure: hook in first 3-5 seconds, problem statement (10-15 sec), solution/product introduction (20-30 sec), benefits with visual examples (30-45 sec), social proof (15-20 sec), strong CTA (10 sec). Constraints: 60-90 seconds total, written for spoken delivery, include [VISUAL CUES] in brackets. Format: two-column script with VIDEO and AUDIO columns. Avoid: long paragraphs hard to read on camera, passive voice, asking to 'like and subscribe' before delivering value.
tone: Energetic and authentic, conversational and direct

name: Create a Checklist
goal: Develop a comprehensive, actionable checklist for task or project completion. Structure: organized by phase or category with clear headings, 15-25 items total, sequential order where dependencies exist. Constraints: each item is a specific action with clear completion criteria, NO emojis. Include: checkbox format, items written as action verbs, dependencies noted, assignee field if team project. Format: □ Action item [Owner] [Deadline] [Notes]. Avoid: combining multiple actions in one item, vague verbs like 'handle', missing critical steps.
tone: Direct and instructional, concise and action-oriented

name: Write an Ad Copy
goal: Craft attention-grabbing advertisement copy using AIDA, PAS, or FAB frameworks. Structure: headline that stops scroll (6-10 words), subheadline expanding on benefit, 2-3 sentences of body copy addressing pain point and solution, strong CTA with urgency or scarcity. Constraints: headline under 10 words, total copy 50-100 words, benefit-focused not feature-focused. Include: specific value proposition, clear CTA, social proof element. Avoid: superlatives without proof, multiple CTAs, negative framing, em dashes.
tone: Bold and persuasive, confident and second-person

name: Generate a Case Study Summary
goal: Summarize case study for business stakeholders using analytical framework. Structure: company/situation background, challenge with quantified impact, solution with specific tactics, measurable results with before/after metrics, key lessons. Constraints: 400-500 words, data-driven and objective, include specific numbers/percentages, NO emojis. Include: ROI metrics, timeline, what worked and what didn't. Format: clear section headers, bullet points for results. Avoid: promotional language, vague outcomes like 'improved efficiency' without numbers.
tone: Analytical and formal, third-person and data-driven

name: Draft a Newsletter
goal: Write engaging newsletter content that drives opens, reads, and clicks. Structure: subject line with preview text suggestion, friendly greeting, brief intro/hook, 3-4 main content sections with clear headings, featured content, CTA section, sign-off with personality. Constraints: 300-500 words, scannable format, mobile-optimized short paragraphs, NO emojis in subject line. Include: personalization elements [FIRST_NAME], relevant links, value-first content before ask. Avoid: ALL CAPS in subject lines, multiple competing CTAs, long blocks of text.
tone: Warm and friendly, conversational like emailing a colleague
